titleStmt.title: National Library of Sweden, A 173
titleStmt.sponsor: Medieval Latin Manuscripts in the National Library: Cataloguing and Digitization
titleStmt.funder: Riksbankens jubileumsfond – The Swedish Foundation for Humanities and Social Sciences

publicationStmt.publisher: manuscripta.se: A Digital Catalogue of Medieval and Early Modern Manuscripts in Sweden

p.note: was originally the pastedown but has come loose from the and has been included among the foliated leaves.

collation.signatures: Quire signatures on quires 4-8 and 10-23. (On quire 5, on the recto of the first leaf, .)

supportDesc.condition: , pastedown heavily worn; wooden boards visible in places, e.g. around the backs of the now-missing boss studs. , boards and board attachments visible due to the detached pastedown. Holes and rust stains left by the bosses.

p.note: Top of page, in pencil by a modern hand: A 173.
Traces of writing in ink, possibly an offprint of a former pastedown.

binding.p: Medieval binding; red leather over wooden boards. 4 raised bands and endbands. Originally, metal corner and centre bosses, front and back. Originally, 2 straps with hooked clasps; decorated metal catchplates on . : in brown ink on a paper label, Missale Romanum ex Bohemia No 19 in 4o ; in brown ink on a paper label, e. 30. 6. 12.

decoNote.p: , panel with blind-tooled pattern of double-line lozenges, lines coloured in with brown ink. , panel with blind-tooled pattern of single-line lozenges, lines coloured in with brown ink.

binding.condition: Leather heavily worn; spine and corners reinforced with brown leather. Straps and clasps missing. All bosses missing, save centre back stud. Holes from strap-fastenings visible on .

origin.p:
The calendar - written by the same hand as the missal rubrics - and contents of the missal indicates Bohemia as the place of origin; St Wenceslaus is celebrated as festum fori, which, according to Grotefend, occurs only in Wrocław, Kraków, Olomouc, and Prague ( Grotefend (1898)
). As in Wrocław and Kraków, St Wenceslaus is also celebrated as festum duplex (Olomouc: solemne); his translation - also observed in Wrocław, Kraków, Olomouc, and Prague - is celebrated on March 8 as festum semiduplex. Notably, however, neither the Passio Ludmille characteristic of Prague and Olomouc (September 16), nor the Ossium recollectio of St Wenceslaus characteristic of Prague (June 27) is included. As in Wrocław and Olomouc, the feast of St Cunigunde is celebrated September 9, but as both festum duplex and festum fori, as are the two feasts of St Augustine (August 28 and October 11). The Dedicatio ecclesie is celebrated on April 16.

In a letter to the National Library dated , Professor Wilhelm Pinder, dates the drawing on to around 1380. Eva Lindqvist Sandgren notes, however, that the artistic style in question still flourished in Bohemia around the year 1400.

Dr Jaroslav Polc of the Pontifical Lateran University dates the manuscript to c. 1390 (note to the National Library stamped ), noting that the feast of Sts Cyril and Methodius, which is an addition to the calendar, was instituted in that time, and that the feast of the Visitation, instituted in 1389, is also an addition. Finally, the feast of St Sigismund, a later addition both to the calendar and the missal text, was instituted in 1356 but not, according to Polc, observed until later.

Finally, in addition to the Dedicatio ecclesie, April 16 is listed in the calendar as Dominica Iubilate, which suggests that the missal was produced in a year when Easter Sunday occurred on March 26, which would mean either or .

bibl.title: Prague: The Crown of Bohemia 1347–1437
bibl.note: According to the authors, the missal includes texts by Jan of Jenštejn, archbishop of Prague 1379 to 1396. These texts have not been identified.

bibl.title: Karel IV. Císař z Boží milosti

note.p: : Sts Cyril and Methodius (9 Mar., addition);

msItem.title: Ordo and Canon missae
msItem.incipit: Dum offeretur hostia et calix dic Panem celestem
msItem.explicit: et pro defunctis communis tamen et cottidiana prefacio dicitur

note.p: The Ordo begins with an instruction for the celebration of Holy Communion followed by prefaces (). The Canon begins on ; no crucifixion image appears to have been included.

is a singleton which has been inserted into the quire; the recto exhibits a drawing of the Virgin Mary with the Christ Child (see Decorations). The verso contains the final part of the blessing of the wine for the feast of St John the Apostle (Benedictio amoris sancti Iohannis); it begins at potencia ac benediccione qua in vltima temporali cena and ends with Bibite et inebriamini dilecti dei ebrietate salutis et prosperitatis corporum et animarum vestrarum et pociamur victoria sempiterna In nomine patris et filii et spriritus sancti amen .

On , a different medieval hand has added three prayers for the blessing of water and salt (inc. Deus qui ad salutem humani generis maxima queque sacramenta in aquarum substantia condidisti), and on , the same hand has added three exorcisms for the blessing of water and salt (inc. Exorciso te creatura salis in nomine dei).

msItem.title: Proper of time
msItem.incipit: AD te leuaui animam meam deus meus (Ps. 24:1)
msItem.explicit: et sempiterne sancte trinitatis eiusdemque indiuidue unitatis confessio Per

note.p: The Proper of time begins with Advent Sunday and ends with Trinity Sunday.
The folium after has been torn out resulting in loss of text.

msItem.title: Votive masses
msItem.explicit: nos ad celestia regna perducat Per

note.p: The section includes the following votive masses: de sancto spiritu (), De sancta cruce (), De domina (), De Angelis (), pro pace (), pro peccatis (), pro tribulatione (), pro humilitate (), of which pro pace, pro tribulatione, and pro humilitate contain the three proper prayers only.
Four leaves are missing after resulting in loss of text.

msItem.title: Proper prayers for mass
msItem.incipit: Pietate tua quesumus domine nostrorum solue uincula delictorum
msItem.explicit: prosint ad ueniam pro quorum quarumque tibi sunt oblata salute Per eundem
msItem.note: Collect, secretum, and postcommunio for general mass.

msItem.title: Instruction for Maundy Thursday and Good Friday mass
msItem.incipit: IN cena domini post nonam prelatus cum ministris vestibus sanctis induti
msItem.explicit: Agnus dei cantabitur quia non secuntur oscula circumstancium Postea secuntur vespere

msItem.title: Dedication of the church
msItem.explicit: ad celestia promissa te ducente peruenire mereatur

msItem.title: Common of saints
msItem.incipit: Michi autem nimis honorati sunt amici tui deus (Ps. 138:17)
msItem.explicit: beati qui audiunt uerbum dei et custodiunt illud (Luke 11:28) Michi autem

note.p: The readings for the different masses are collected into one section extending from to .

msItem.title: Proper of saints
msItem.explicit: Refecti participacione muneris sacri quesumus domine deus noster ut cuius exequimur cultum senciamus effectum Per

note.p: The Proper of saints begins with the feast of St Andrew (30 November) and ends with the commemoration of St Linus (26 November).
For the majority of Saints' feasts, only the three proper prayers are included.
: St Sigismund (2 May; added in margin);

msItem.title: Prayers for the masses of the Virgin Mary
msItem.incipit: Deus qui de beate marie uirginis utero

msItem.title: Mass for the dead
msItem.incipit: Inclina domine aurem tuam ad preces nostras
msItem.explicit: Qui manducat hunc panem uiuet in eternum
msItem.note: The section lists, first, the proper prayers for different categories of deceased, and ends with a complete mass formulary beginning on .

msItem.title: Mass of Corpus Christi
msItem.explicit: quia preciosi corporis et sanguinis tui temporalis precepcio prefigurat Per dominum

msItem.title: Mass of the Holy Lance and Nails (Lancea Domini)
msItem.incipit: Foderunt manus meas et pedes meos (Ps. 21:17)
msItem.explicit: oblacio ueneranda indulgenciam nobis obtineat et gloriam sempiternam Qui cum patre

msItem.title: Sequences
msItem.explicit: coram agno coram throno laudes super ethera Amen

msItem.title: Prayers before Mass
msItem.incipit: Summe sacerdos et uere pontifex qui te obtulisti
msItem.explicit: ita ut nec esuriam neque siciam amplius in eternum Amen dicant omnia nunc et semper et in secula seculorum AmEN

msItem.title: Miscellaneous mass items
msItem.incipit: De aduentu domini prima missa Ad te leuaui Secunda puer natus
msItem.explicit: desideriis accensi fonte uite senciamus ihesum christum dominum nostrum
msItem.note: Miscellaneous mass items (e.g. introits, alleluias, prayers) in two different hands.

msItem.title: Miscellaneous mass items
msItem.incipit: Venit ihesus in partes cesaree philippi (Matt. 16:13)
msItem.explicit: ille vero baptizabit vos Spiritu Sancto (Mark 1:8)

note.p: Miscellaneous mass items (e.g. gospel readings and prayers) in at least 5 different hands.
A leaf is missing after resulting in loss of text.

supportDesc.condition: Some holes, stains, and dirt, in particular around the edges and lower corners. Some water damage to , , , , and , though only minor damage to the legibility of the text. Minor loss of text in the marginal additions reveals that the leaves have been trimmed.

layout.note: Guidelines in drypoint and ink. Ruling in drypoint; in ink, seven staves of four lines each with space for text in between.
Ruling in drypoint, ; ruling in ink .
The ruling changes mid-page of and the original spacing is halved. ends with four musical staves written into the regular ruling.
Eight staves of four lines each ruled in ink (nine on ). The text is written on the upper line of the following stave.
Seven staves of four lines each (six on ) and one line ruled for the text below each stave.

handDesc.p: Gothic textualis. Identification of individual hands tentative.

p.note: Top of page, in ink by an early modern hand: Missale.
Below last line, alternative line of preface added in a medieval hand.
Prayers and exorcisms for the blessing of water and salt in the same medieval hand.
Marginal annotations in ink by a medieval hand indicating the mass item or contents of passage.
Various mass items (mostly prayers, some biblical passages of varying length, and the occasional hymn) added in the margin by various medieval hands, e.g. .
Small ink drawings, the majority of them portraits of a woman (some of which possibly the Virgin Mary).
Miscellaneous notes. Top of page, in a cursive hand, brief notes on three offices for St Nicholas: Officium de sancto Nicolao.
Centre page: in brown ink by a medieval hand, pen trials and the note Wir albrecht von gotes genam hertzog, and another note possibly also in German.

musicNotation.p: : staves of four lines in brown ink with square notation. : staves of 4 lines in brown ink with square notation.

decoDesc.p: Main text in brown and black ink; rubrics in red; capitals touched in red, some with additional penwork.

decoNote.p: : puzzle section initial T over 6 lines in brown and red ink with flourishing in red in counterspace and from top to bottom of margin. : puzzle section initial A (in foliage patterns) over 9 lines in brown and red ink with flourishing in red and brown in counterspace and from top to bottom of margin.
: plain initials over 1-7 lines in red ink, a few with flourishing in brown ink (e.g. ).
: plain initials in brown or black ink, one with penwork.
